2026-04-09T03:23:38.6664827Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T03:21:40.543298912Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57973,\"title\":\"[Azure AI Agents Persistent] Docs: CHANGELOG missing entries for TypeSpec emitter migration in 1.2.0-beta.11\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57631) by `@radhgupta`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57631 migrated `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration entailed:\\n\\n- **Complete regeneration** of all files under `src/Generated/` (dozens of files removed and re-added under the new emitter output)\\n- **New backward-compatibility shims** added under `src/Custom/BackwardCompat/`:\\n  - `AbstractTypeConstructors.cs` — constructors for abstract types\\n  - `ClientMethodShims.cs` — overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` metadata (old signature) delegating to the new `IDictionary` signatures on `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, and `VectorStores`\\n  - `MissingTypes.cs` — types present in old generated output but missing from new generator output\\n  - `ModelFactoryShims.cs` — shims for `PersistentAgentsModelFactory` factory methods\\n  - `PropertyShims.cs` — property-level backward compat shims\\n- **Renamed client builder extension**: `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` → `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`\\n- **New generated model factory**: `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` (2372 lines added)\\n- **API surface change**: ~970 additions and ~145 deletions in the public API listing files\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `CHANGELOG.md` unreleased section `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` is completely empty — all four subsections (`Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, `Other Changes`) are blank despite significant changes\\n- The metadata parameter type change (`IReadOnlyDictionary` → `IDictionary`) is a **breaking change** that is not documented\\n- The renamed `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (formerly `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`) is not mentioned in the changelog\\n- The new `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` type is not documented in the changelog\\n- The new `BackwardCompat` shims that preserve compatibility for callers using the old `IReadOnlyDictionary` metadata parameter type are not mentioned\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section to add entries for all changes introduced in the emitter migration. Suggested content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` as the new centralized model factory for test and mock support.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n- Metadata parameters across `PersistentAgentsAdministrationClient`, `ThreadMessages`, `ThreadRuns`, `Threads`, and `VectorStores` changed from `IReadOnlyDictionary` to `IDictionary`. Backward-compatible overloads accepting `IReadOnlyDictionary` are provided as `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]` shims for existing callers.\\n- `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` was renamed to `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n- Migrated package code generation to the new TypeSpec HTTP client C# emitter. No functional behavior change is expected for most users.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippets are required for this change; the CHANGELOG update is pure documentation.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections. The migration entailed:\\n\\n- **Complete regeneration** of all files under `src/Generated/` (dozens of files removed and re-added under the new emitter output)\\n- **New backward-compatibility shims** added under `src/Custom/BackwardCompat/`:\\n  - `MissingTypes.cs` — types present in old generated output but missing from new generator output\\n  - `ModelFactoryShims.cs` — shims for `PersistentAgentsModelFactory` factory methods\\n  - `PropertyShims.cs` — property-level backward compat shims\\n- **Renamed client builder extension**: `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` → `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`\\n- **New generated model factory**: `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` (2372 lines added)\\n- **API surface change**: ~970 additions and ~145 deletions in the public API listing files\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `CHANGELOG.md` unreleased section `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` is completely empty — all four subsections (`Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, `Other Changes`) are blank despite significant changes\\n- The metadata parameter type change (`IReadOnlyDictionary` → `IDictionary`) is a **breaking change** that is not documented\\n- The renamed `AgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions` (formerly `AIAgentsPersistentClientBuilderExtensions`) is not mentioned in the changelog\\n- The new `AgentsPersistentModelFactory` type is not documented in the changelog\\n- The new `BackwardCompat` shims that preserve compatibility for callers using the old `IReadOnlyDictionary` metadata parameter type are not mentioned\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `## 1.2.0-beta.11 (Unreleased)` section to add entries for all changes introduced in the emitter migration. 2026-04-09T03:23:38.6833677Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T03:21:55.217428231Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":24,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent OpenTelemetryScope throws if arguments are invalid JSON\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.5\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nFrom time to time we observe an issue with FunctionCalls where the Arguments seems to contain illegal JSON that can not be deserialized, which in turn throws an Exception in the `ProcessToolCalls` function of `OpenTelemetryScope`.\\n\\nhttps://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Telemetry/OpenTelemetryScope.cs#L1147\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n* The issue (I would say) is that there is an assumption on arguments to be valid JSON. \\n* The error should not occur in the `OpenTelemetryScope`, before the user has any chance to handle it.\\n* Also, shouldn't agents enfore valid JSON responses? (structured outputs or something, am I doing something wrong?)\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nAn exception when function call arguments are deserialized.\\n\\n```\\nThe JSON value could not be converted to System.String. Path: $.repeatable | LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 64.\\n\\nat System.Text.Json.ThrowHelper.ReThrowWithPath(ReadStack\\u0026 state, Utf8JsonReader\\u0026 reader, Exception ex)\\nat Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\nat Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.AsyncStreamingUpdateEnumerator.DisposeAsyncCore()\\nat Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.AsyncStreamingUpdateCollection.AsyncStreamingUpdateEnumerator.DisposeAsync()\\nat Services.AzureOpenAiClient.ProcessRunAsync(AsyncCollectionResult`1 responseStream, String userKey, String threadId, ThreadRun run, FormSnapshot activeForm, CancellationToken cancellationToken)+MoveNext()\\n\\n```\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nEvery time the agents calls a function but passes invalid JSON, the code in our codebase that fails looks like this:\\n\\n```csharp\\nasync IAsyncEnumerable\\u003cAssistantStreamingUpdate\\u003e ProcessRunAsync(\\n    AsyncCollectionResult\\u003cStreamingUpdate\\u003e responseStream, string threadId, ThreadRun? run,\\n    [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n{\\n    do\\n    {\\n        List\\u003cToolOutput\\u003e toolOutputs = [];\\n\\n        await foreach (var streamingUpdate in responseStream) // this throws\\n        {\\n            // we never get here\\n        }\\n\\n        if (toolOutputs.Count \\u003e 0)\\n        {\\n            responseStream =\\n                _persistentAgentsClient.Runs.SubmitToolOutputsToStreamAsync(run, toolOutputs, cancellationToken);\\n            continue;\\n        }\\n\\n        break;\\n    } while (true);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nI can not wrap it inside a try catch since inside th foreach I am yielding individual parts back over the API:\\n```csharp\\n// `processUserMessageAsync` is the IAsyncEnumerable\\u003cAssistantStreamingUpdate\\u003e\\nawait foreach (var update in processUserMessageAsync.WithCancellation(cancellationToken))\\n{\\n    await
